burden.mortalityRate: Low; higher in TB or purulent cases

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: At least two of: pleuritic chest pain, pericardial rub, typical ECG changes, or pericardial effusion

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening; diagnosis in emergency settings
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate; recurrence and delayed care are common

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: ECG and echo mainly in urban/tertiary centers
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: NSAIDs plus colchicine; anti-TB therapy for TB cases
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: TB burden, delayed presentation, and limited imaging in rural areas
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Established TB programs and growing cardiology services
